mechanism_narrative: Sustained elevation of the federal funds rate above 4.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that increases the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as equity valuations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.33% as of decision date
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS > 4.0% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.2% as of decision date
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following UNRATE increases > 0.3pp are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales (RSAFS) combined with falling housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 722708.0M and HOUST at 1501.0K as of decision date
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following joint RSAFS and HOUST declines do not show negative bias (mean return not significantly < 0, t-test p > 0.05), or interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 3-month moving average signals supply-side weakness that constrains profit margins and reduces earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.2062 as of decision date
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns when INDPRO is below its 3-month MA do not show negative bias (mean return not significantly < 0, t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) combined with stable or declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals tight labor market conditions that increase wage pressures and corporate labor costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as profit margins compress.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS grows > 200K month-over-month AND UNRATE remains < 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 159398K and UNRATE at 4.2% as of decision date
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage inflation risk
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during periods of strong PAYEMS growth and low UNRATE do not show negative bias (mean return not significantly < 0, t-test p > 0.05), or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 3.5% year-over-year combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4.0%) signals sustained monetary restriction that dampens consumer demand and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 319.615 and FEDFUNDS at 4.33% as of decision date
regime_hint: stagflation risk regime with monetary tightening
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during high inflation and high rates do not show negative bias (mean return not significantly < 0, t-test p > 0.05), or the joint effect is weaker than either single-series effect (interaction p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating below 2.0% annualized signals weakening economic expansion that reduces corporate earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 23542.349B (chained 2017 dollars) as of decision date
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following quarters with GDPC1 growth < 2.0% are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) of more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period signals monetary easing that reduces discount rates and increases equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.33% as of decision date
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle or policy pivot
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines > 0.5pp are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
